key_items: Bitcoin sold off to $76,000 with bears reclaiming control as price falls under $77K amid weak US demand
Mark Cuban sells most Bitcoin calling it a failed hedge; analyst predicts potential $50,000-$55,000 target after breaking 14-year support
Kalshi prediction market shows Bitcoin $100K probability dropped from 40% to zero as traders turn bearish; monthly Bollinger Bands at tightest levels signal volatility ahead
reasoning: Multiple bearish technical signals converge with weakening sentiment: price breakdown below key support levels, institutional skepticism (Cuban's exit, Goldman Sachs XRP dump), and prediction market traders aggressively reducing 2026 bull run probability. Positive catalysts like institutional adoption (Tether, Nasdaq-Polymarket) and strategic reserve legislation offer some support but are overwhelmed by near-term technical weakness and negative sentiment shift.
risks: Accelerating breakdown through $76,000 support could cascade liquidations toward $60,000-$50,000 targets if technical trendline resistance continues holding; regulatory uncertainty around prediction markets and tokenized stocks adds unpredictability.
